summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the HTTP Stream transport reads arbitrarily large request bodies into memory without enforcing the configured maxMessageSize limit. A constrained-heap server process is killed by V8 OOM when a large body is sent to the /mcp endpoint.
expected_security_impact: After the attack, the MCP server process should be killed by V8 out-of-memory or become completely unresponsive, and the /health endpoint should no longer return 200 OK.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched a minimal MCP server with HTTP Stream transport on a 64 MB heap limit. The server responded to GET /health with {"ok":true} before the attack. An 8 MB body (exceeding the 4 MB maxMessageSize config) was accepted without rejection, proving the limit is dead. A subsequent 128 MB body caused V8 to terminate the process with an OOM error. The post-attack health check confirmed the server was no longer reachable.
verification_method: The run script checks (1) server health returns 200 OK before the attack, (2) the 8 MB oversized body is accepted rather than rejected with a size-limit error, confirming maxMessageSize is not enforced, and (3) server health fails after the large body attack, confirming the DoS impact. The script uses a 64 MB heap limit via --max-old-space-size=64 to make the OOM deterministic within the Docker container.
evidence_produced_by_run: GET /health returned {"ok":true} before the attack, confirming the server was operational.
POST /mcp with an 8 MB body completed without a size-limit rejection, proving maxMessageSize is dead configuration.
POST /mcp with a 128 MB body caused the Node.js process to exit with a FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit or CALL_AND_RETRY_LAST allocation failure OOM signal.
GET /health after the attack failed to connect, confirming the server was terminated.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
